what_it_does: Configures the Claude Code harness for this repo: sets worktree background isolation to 'none' and registers a single SessionStart hook that runs ops/hooks/cc_registry_freshness_check.sh as a shell command at the start of every session.
observations: One SessionStart hook of type 'command' invoking an absolute-path bash script: /home/tim/company/ops/hooks/cc_registry_freshness_check.sh
No permissions, env, or other hook events declared
standouts: A registry-freshness check is wired to fire automatically at session start — orientation is enforced by the harness, not left to the agent
conventions: Uses an absolute path to the hook script rather than a relative one
notes: The repo's Claude Code config — its only active behavior is a session-start registry freshness check via a bash hook.
questions: What does cc_registry_freshness_check.sh check and what happens on a stale registry?
Why is bgIsolation set to 'none' — what isolation behavior is being opted out of?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a (no stated purpose in-file; behavior is self-evident config)
intention_signals: Make every session begin by checking the live registry is fresh, so agents orient before acting rather than working from a stale self-model.
novelty: Tying a registry-freshness gate to the SessionStart hook event is a deliberate orientation enforcement mechanism.
contribution: Contributes the harness-level wiring that auto-runs a registry freshness check at session start and disables worktree background isolation.
summary_for_embedding: Claude Code settings.json registering a SessionStart bash hook for registry freshness and setting worktree bgIsolation to none.
intention_for_embedding: To enforce registry-freshness orientation automatically at the start of every Claude Code session in this repo.
